title: V2 Read Notes

source.note: No live keel CLI or keel-plan skill is installed in this environment; this binder follows the KEEL work-item contract shape used by prior repo artifacts.

included: Reader pane for the selected mock note
Rendered markdown body below the existing title, metadata, and tags
Markdown prose styling for headings, paragraphs, emphasis, lists, blockquotes, tables, code, horizontal rules, and task-list checkboxes
Safe markdown-to-HTML rendering using established libraries rather than handwritten parsing
Tests that prove the first H1 is not duplicated and markdown structure is preserved
excluded: Editing workflow and raw markdown editor
Search/sidebar/tag-filter behavior beyond existing tag click integration
Mermaid rendering unless already supported by the chosen markdown-rendering implementation without broadening scope

design_facts: Prototype ReadView renders the note title separately, then renders stripTitle(note.content) as markdown in a `.prose` container.
Prototype uses `marked.parse` for markdown rendering and styles `.prose` for headings, blockquotes, lists, tables, code, and task-list checkboxes.
Current V2 app on feat/parchmark-v2 renders read content with `plainPreview(activeNote.content)` inside a single `.note-body` paragraph, flattening markdown markers.
Current mock note data already contains headings, emphasis, blockquotes, unordered lists, ordered lists, GFM tables, and task-list markdown.
invariants: Content is king: the read pane must prioritize the user's writing and avoid toolbar or decoration creep.
Server/backend scope is unchanged: this slice uses existing local mock notes only.
Markdown helpers must preserve existing `extractTitle`, `stripTitle`, `plainPreview`, `wordCount`, and `readingTime` semantics unless a work item explicitly amends them.
New custom CSS must consume project tokens from `ui/src/design-system/tokens.css`; no copied prototype CSS literals when an existing semantic/component token exists.

work_items.title: Render markdown in the V2 read pane

contract.summary: Replace the flattened `.note-body` preview in `AppShell.vue` with a dedicated read-view markdown renderer that keeps the existing title/meta/tag chrome but renders `stripTitle(activeNote.content)` as structured markdown.

required: Use a proven markdown renderer, preferably `marked` to match the prototype behavior.
Use an HTML sanitizer, preferably `dompurify`, before rendering HTML with `v-html`.
forbidden: Do not hand-roll a markdown parser.
Do not render unsanitized user-authored markdown through `v-html`.
Do not add network calls or backend endpoints.

component_behavior: `MarkdownProse` accepts raw note markdown and renders the title-stripped body.
`MarkdownProse` renders inside a `.prose` container in the existing document measure.
The read pane still shows `doc-title`, reading time, word count, edited time, and clickable tag chips above the markdown body.
The first H1 in note content is removed from the markdown body so the title is not duplicated.
Markdown headings render as headings, blockquotes render as blockquotes, list items remain list items, GFM tables render as tables, and task-list checkboxes render as disabled checkboxes.
styling: Add V2 prose styles in the owning read component or a small co-located style block.
Use existing project tokens such as `--text`, `--text-2`, `--accent`, `--surface-2`, `--line`, `--line-2`, `--serif`, and `--shadow-sm`.
Preserve current read-pane measure and document title rhythm unless validation proves the design requires a small adjustment.

assertions: Read view of `Morning Pages` renders a `What it's for` heading and a `Today` heading below the title.
The rendered markdown body does not include a duplicate H1 `Morning Pages` heading.
The blockquote text `The page asks for nothing back. That's the whole trick.` renders inside a `blockquote` element.
The three Morning Pages rules render as list items, not as a flattened paragraph.
The `Reading list` note renders its markdown table as a `table` with header cells for `Title`, `Author`, and `Why`.
The `Standup notes` note renders task-list markdown as disabled checkboxes and keeps checked state for `[x]`.
A malicious markdown fixture containing script/event-handler HTML is sanitized before DOM insertion.
Existing search, tag filtering, note selection, title extraction, word count, and reading time tests remain green.
Design validation against the prototype read view has zero critical and zero major discrepancies for the read-pane content structure.
oracle.setup: Run the V2 Vue app with mock notes, select notes from the sidebar, and exercise the read mode.
oracle.tooling: Vitest + Vue Test Utils for unit/component coverage; Kargha validate or equivalent Playwright validation for visual structure.

focus_areas: spacing between title, metadata, rule, and body
safe absence of raw markdown markers in rendered body

notes: This work item intentionally does not depend on the header-topbar PR. If that PR lands first, keep its extracted `AppTopbar` shape; otherwise adapt only the read-pane section in `AppShell.vue`.
Because this introduces markdown-to-HTML rendering, sanitizer coverage is part of the contract, not an optional hardening follow-up.
